body_json.description:
Senior Full-Stack Development Team Needed – National Fleet & Heavy Equipment Management Platform
Project Overview
We are seeking an experienced software development company or senior development team to design, develop, implement, and support a large-scale Fleet and Heavy Equipment Management Platform for a public-sector infrastructure program.
The platform will manage government-owned heavy-duty construction and infrastructure equipment deployed across multiple regions and project sites. The solution will serve as a centralized system for asset tracking, maintenance management, workforce management, project monitoring, fuel tracking, reporting, and real-time GPS monitoring.
This is a complex enterprise-grade project requiring experience in fleet management, asset management, ERP systems, GIS/GPS integrations, mobile applications, and cloud-based architectures.
 
Project Objectives
The platform should provide:
•	Centralized management of heavy equipment assets 
•	Real-time equipment visibility and monitoring 
•	Deployment and assignment tracking 
•	Preventive and corrective maintenance management 
•	Workforce and certification management 
•	Fuel consumption and cost tracking 
•	Vendor and contractor management 
•	Project monitoring and utilization reporting 
•	Executive dashboards and analytics 
•	Mobile field operations with offline capability 
•	GPS and telematics integration 
 
Scope of Work
The successful bidder will be responsible for:
1. Requirements Validation & Solution Design
•	Business requirements review 
•	Technical architecture design 
•	Database design 
•	User journey mapping 
•	UI/UX wireframes 
•	Security architecture 
 
2. Web-Based Management Portal
Develop a responsive web platform with the following modules:
Asset Management
•	Asset registration 
•	Asset categorization 
•	Digital asset profiles 
•	Equipment specifications 
•	Document management 
•	QR code generation 
•	RFID readiness 
Deployment & Assignment
•	Equipment requests 
•	Approval workflows 
•	Assignment tracking 
•	Utilization monitoring 
•	Accountability records 
Maintenance Management
•	Preventive maintenance scheduling 
•	Corrective maintenance tracking 
•	Work orders 
•	Spare parts tracking 
•	Service history 
•	Maintenance alerts 
Workforce Management
•	Operator registry 
•	Engineer registry 
•	Certification tracking 
•	License management 
•	Performance monitoring 
•	Attendance management 
Fuel Management
•	Fuel issuance records 
•	Fuel consumption monitoring 
•	Cost analysis 
•	Fuel anomaly detection 
Vendor & Contractor Management
•	Vendor onboarding 
•	Contract tracking 
•	SLA monitoring 
•	Performance evaluations 
Project Monitoring
•	Project registration 
•	Equipment allocation 
•	Productivity tracking 
•	Resource utilization reporting 
Reporting & Analytics
•	Executive dashboards 
•	Operational reporting 
•	Cost analysis 
•	Utilization reports 
•	Maintenance compliance reports 
•	Custom reporting tools 
 
3. GPS & Telematics Module
The platform should support:
•	Real-time GPS tracking 
•	Live map visualization 
•	Geofencing 
•	Route history 
•	Engine hour monitoring 
•	Utilization tracking 
•	Idle time monitoring 
•	Unauthorized movement alerts 
Developers should indicate experience integrating with GPS and telematics providers.
 
4. Mobile / Tablet Application
Develop a mobile application for Android tablets and field personnel.
Required features:
•	Offline functionality 
•	Data synchronization when connectivity is restored 
•	Incident reporting 
•	Daily operational reporting 
•	Fuel logging 
•	Maintenance logging 
•	Photo capture and upload 
•	Document uploads 
•	Equipment assignment management 
 
5. Notifications & Alerts
The system should provide:
•	Maintenance reminders 
•	Fuel anomaly alerts 
•	Equipment idle alerts 
•	Unauthorized movement alerts 
•	Certification expiry alerts 
•	Assignment notifications 
Support for:
•	Email notifications 
•	SMS notifications 
•	In-app notifications 
 
Technical Requirements
Developers may recommend technologies; however, preference will be given to modern, scalable architectures.
Frontend
•	React.js 
•	Angular 
•	Next.js 
Mobile
•	Flutter 
•	React Native 
Backend
•	.NET Core 
•	Node.js 
•	Java Spring Boot 
Database
•	PostgreSQL 
•	MS SQL Server 
Cloud Infrastructure
•	Microsoft Azure 
•	AWS 
GIS / Mapping
•	Google Maps 
•	Mapbox 
•	OpenStreetMap 
APIs
•	REST APIs 
•	Webhooks 
•	Third-party integrations 
 
Security Requirements
The solution must include:
•	Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) 
•	Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) 
•	Audit logs 
•	Encryption at rest 
•	Encryption in transit 
•	Backup and recovery capabilities 
•	Disaster recovery planning 
•	Secure API architecture 
 
Expected User Roles
The platform should support multiple user types including:
•	System Administrators 
•	Regional Managers 
•	Operations Coordinators 
•	Maintenance Managers 
•	Workshop Personnel 
•	Equipment Operators 
•	Contractors/Vendors 
•	Auditors 
•	Executive Users 
 
Deliverables
The selected team will be expected to provide:
Discovery Phase
•	Requirements documentation 
•	Functional specifications 
•	Technical architecture 
•	Wireframes 
Development Phase
•	Backend platform 
•	Frontend portal 
•	Mobile application 
•	APIs and integrations 
Testing Phase
•	Unit testing 
•	System testing 
•	User Acceptance Testing (UAT) 
•	Security testing 
Deployment Phase
•	Production deployment 
•	Training 
•	Documentation 
•	Knowledge transfer 
Post-Go-Live Support
•	Warranty support 
•	Bug fixes 
•	Maintenance support options 
 
Required Experience
Applicants must demonstrate experience in at least one of the following:
•	Fleet Management Systems 
•	Enterprise Asset Management (EAM) 
•	ERP Solutions 
•	Government Systems 
•	Logistics Platforms 
•	Telematics Solutions 
•	GIS-Based Applications 
•	Offline Mobile Applications 
Preference will be given to teams that can provide live demonstrations, case studies, or references from similar projects.
 
Proposal Submission Requirements
Please include:
Company / Team Profile
•	Years in business 
•	Team structure 
•	Location 
Relevant Experience
Provide examples of similar projects including:
•	Project description 
•	Technology stack 
•	Client industry 
•	Screenshots or demo links 
Technical Proposal
Include:
•	Recommended architecture 
•	Technology stack 
•	Development methodology 
•	Security approach 
4. Project Timeline
Provide estimated duration for:
•	Discovery 
•	Development 
•	Testing 
•	Deployment 
5. Cost Proposal
Provide:
•	Total estimated project cost 
•	Milestone-based pricing 
•	Support and maintenance pricing 
 
Evaluation Criteria
Proposals will be evaluated based on:
•	Relevant experience 
•	Technical approach 
•	Scalability of solution 
•	Security architecture 
•	Delivery methodology 
•	Cost competitiveness 
•	Post-implementation support capability 
 
Budget
Budget: Open to Proposals
Developers are invited to submit fixed-price or milestone-based proposals. The project owner is seeking the best combination of technical capability, experience, scalability, and value.
 
Additional Requirements
The selected development partner must:
•	Provide complete source code ownership upon project completion. 
•	Deliver comprehensive technical and user documentation. 
•	Support cloud deployment and production rollout. 
•	Participate in stakeholder review sessions. 
•	Provide a minimum warranty/support period after go-live. 
•	Be available for periodic virtual meetings during development. 
 
Application Instructions
When applying, please include the following:
1.	Brief introduction of your company/team. 
2.	Similar projects completed within the last five years. 
3.	Proposed technology stack. 
4.	Estimated timeline. 
5.	Estimated budget range. 
6.	Team composition and key personnel. 
7.	Links to live systems, demos, or portfolios (if available).
body_json.title: Fleet & Heavy Equipment Management Platform

body_json.description:
Part-Time to Full-Time

About Concierge Drop
Concierge Drop is a premium on-demand home services company operating inside NYC luxury residential buildings. Residents book dry cleaning, home cleaning, dog walking, uniform cleaning, and more directly from our website — picked up and delivered from the building lobby. No app required.

We're in 200+ buildings across Manhattan and growing fast, with a 2026 goal of 150 new buildings. As we scale, our operations need an owner — someone who keeps vendors, systems, and processes running cleanly behind the scenes so the rest of the team can focus on growth.

The Role
You are the operational backbone of the business. You run the machinery that makes service delivery possible: vendor onboarding, compliance, the systems that run our orders, and the documented procedures that keep everything consistent as we grow.

This is a behind-the-scenes role for someone who loves order, hates loose ends, and gets satisfaction from turning chaos into a clean, repeatable system. You'll work closely with the Account Coordinator and report into leadership.

What You Own:

- Vendor Onboarding
Bring new vendors into the system cleanly and completely. Collect required documentation, set them up in our platforms, confirm service areas and capabilities, and make sure every vendor is ready to take orders before they go live.
- Compliance
Keep vendor and operational records current and in good standing. Track documentation, flag anything expiring or missing, and make sure we stay compliant across the portfolio. Nothing lapses on your watch.
- Systems & Technical Operations
Own the day-to-day inside CleanCloud and Launch 27 — configuration, order routing, troubleshooting, and keeping data clean. You're the person who knows how these systems actually work and can fix things when they break.
- Account Coordination Support
Work hand-in-hand with the Account Coordinator. When a building needs a vendor assigned, a service set up, or an operational issue resolved, you make it happen on the backend.
- Standard Operating Procedures & The Runbook
Build and maintain our internal runbook so that how we do things lives in documentation, not in someone's head. Standardize procedures, reduce knowledge silos, and make sure any process you run can be picked up by someone else if needed.

Who You Are
- Open to candidates who can overlap 4+ hours with EST
- Excellent written and spoken English — you communicate clearly with vendors and teammates
- Highly systems-oriented — you pick up new software fast and enjoy getting it configured right
- Detail-obsessed — documentation, compliance, and data accuracy come naturally to you
- Process-minded — you instinctively turn one-off tasks into repeatable procedures
- Proactive — you flag problems before they escalate, not after
- Experience in operations, vendor management, or a coordination-heavy role
- Comfortable with operational software, Google Sheets, and Slack — experience with CleanCloud or Launch 27 a strong plus
- Thinks like a business owner, not an employee

This role is NOT for you if:
- You need constant direction to stay productive
- You treat this as a low-commitment side job
- You're uncomfortable owning systems and being the go-to person when something breaks
- You'd rather follow a process than build one

Compensation & Growth

- Rate: $5 per hour (based on operations/systems experience)
- Part-time to start, with a clear path to full-time based on performance
- We build long-term with our remote team. Our CS Manager is has been with us since the beginning

How to apply:
- A short note (3–5 sentences) on your operations or vendor management experience
- One example of a process you documented or improved — what was broken, and what did you change?
body_json.title: Operations Manager / Coordinator — Concierge Drop

body_json.description:
Full Charge Bookkeeper
Ongoing | QuickBooks Online | Profit First
---

About the Engagement

U.S. company seeks a skilled, proactive Full Charge Bookkeeper to own our complete bookkeeping function — independently and with high ownership.

This is a long-term, ongoing engagement. We're not looking for someone to fill a transactional role. We're looking for a trusted provider who takes initiative, surfaces issues before we ask, and keeps our books clean enough to rely on.
---

What You'll Own

**Accounts Receivable — Top Priority**
- Monitor and apply client payments daily; reconcile all billing channels (automated charges + manual invoices)
- Enforce our prepay policy and manage our tiered collections process using the provided scripts
- Create new client invoices upon signed agreement; send 15 days before due date

**Accounts Payable**
- Manage vendor payables on our 10th and 25th payment cycle (Profit First)
- Maintain complete vendor records in QuickBooks Bill Pay; process ACH and mailed check payments
- Verify Profit First automated account transfers on the 5th and 20th of each month

**Monthly Close**
- Fully reconcile all accounts and confirm books closed to our ownership and CPA firm by the 12th of each month
- Download and share bank statements with our tax strategist CPA firm by the 5th
- Attend monthly Zoom virtual CFO call with our tax strategist CPA firm 

**Client & Vendor Onboarding**
- Set up new client and vendor records in QBO upon receipt of signed agreements
- Manage prompt payment setup
---

## Requirements

- **QuickBooks Online:** Proficient — this is non-negotiable
- **Profit First:** Familiar with the methodology or willing to learn quickly
- **English:** Clear, fluent, spoken, and written — you will occasionally join calls with our leadership team, CPA firm, and maybe clients
- **Availability:** Reachable during U.S. Eastern business hours for occasional calls; same or next business day response to all messages
- **Assignments:** Completed within 1–2 business days
- **Attitude:** Proactive. You surface issues. You check QBO history before asking questions. You read related agreements before asking someone to explain it.
---

## Scope & Compensation

- **Hours:** 5–15 hours per week (fluctuates with business activity)
- **Contract type:** Ongoing hourly
- **Location:** Fully remote
- **Rate:** Competitive; commensurate with experience
---
questions:
Rate your spoken English clarity for U.S. business calls (1–5):
1 = Heavy accent 2 = Noticeable 3 = Moderate 4 = Light 5 = Native/near-native.
What does "books are closed" mean to you? Walk us through what you verify before confirming close.
What is your availability during U.S. Eastern business hours, and how quickly do you typically respond to messages?
Briefly describe a situation where you caught a billing or payment error proactively — before you were asked to look for it.
How do you handle a transaction you don't recognize in QBO?
body_json.title: Seeking a bookkeeper

body_json.description:
Company is a high-velocity, remote-first, global team changing the face of wellbeing and stress management. If you’re passionate about making a positive impact in the world and you play a driving role in a team, this is an exciting opportunity where your efforts will clearly make a difference. 

You will serve as a critical execution partner to our CEO, charged with making things happen - from creating a system for engaging guest composers, to accelerating project completion, to architecting our internal data systems and administrative overhauls.  You are a proactive "blocker-remover" who can take a vague directive, identify potential hurdles, and see a project through to a high-quality finish with minimal oversight. This is the place where your achievement focus serves a global mission.

Core Responsibilities

Strategic Execution & CEO Leverage
* Agile-style Project Management: Lead end-to-end execution of high-priority initiatives. Initial focus areas include project managing our website needs, App design upgrade & development, and new marketing initiatives. You must be ready to pivot as company priorities evolve.
* Executive Momentum & Follow-up: Act as the "Conductor" for the executive team. You will track and drive the completion of critical actions from the CEO, CFO, and other leads throughout the week, ensuring that by the time the Management Team meets, "in-progress" items have actually moved forward.
* High-Stakes Stakeholder Management: Communicate across the C-suite with confidence and diplomacy. You are responsible for "managing up"—proactively chasing updates and ensuring the CEO, CFO, and partners stay on track with their commitments.

Knowledge Architecture & Systems Building

* Information Management: Architect and maintain our "Single Source of Truth." This includes ongoing (re)-organization and curation of our Google Drive (400+ files) into an intuitive, indexed asset library.
* Business Intelligence (BI): Navigate our data platforms (website, App analytics, etc.) to provide clarity. When a question arises, you analyze data, check with teammates, identify trends, and provide recommendations, demonstrating operational rigor, data literacy, and startup agility to build systems from scratch.
* Process Codification: Work with the appropriate teams to turn recurring tasks (template use, vendor onboarding) into repeatable, scalable playbooks.

Operational Risk & Support

* Business & Commercial Literacy: While not a legal role, you "think in legal terms"—identifying when decisions require formal documentation, when contracts need closer scrutiny, and how to protect company assets through organized record-keeping.
* Autonomous Research: Follow a "Research → Recommend → Execute" framework. You identify the best tools or paths forward, explain the "Why" to the CEO, and execute once signed off.

Required Qualifications

* 4+ years of Project Management in a fast-paced, high-growth startup environment (0-to-1 or Series A experience highly preferred).
* Exceptional Communication: You are comfortable "managing up" and holding senior leaders accountable to deadlines without creating friction.
* The "Natural Organizer": You find genuine satisfaction in turning digital chaos into structured, searchable, and scalable systems.
* Data Savviness: Expert-level proficiency in Google Workspace and a knack for navigating data dashboards to extract actionable insights.
* Independent Drive: Ability to work with forensic attention to detail while knowing when to proactively seek clarification to guide next steps.
questions: Describe your recent experience with similar projects

body_json.description:
PROJECT TITLE:
Houston Commercial Property & Recurring Maintenance Prospect Database

PROJECT DESCRIPTION:

I own and operate HomeSmiles Pearland, an exterior maintenance company in the Houston, Texas area. I am looking for a lead generation/data research specialist to build a highly organized commercial prospect database focused on recurring maintenance opportunities.

This is NOT a generic business lead list project.

I need a curated database of property-focused businesses and properties that are likely to outsource recurring exterior maintenance services to local owner-operated vendors.

SERVICES WE PROVIDE:
- Pressure washing
- Soft washing
- Breezeway cleaning
- Dumpster pad cleaning
- Sidewalk cleaning
- Exterior window cleaning
- Gutter cleaning
- Dryer vent cleaning
- Common area cleaning
- Exterior preventative maintenance

TARGET MARKET AREAS:
Houston metro area including:
- Pearland
- Friendswood
- League City
- Webster
- Clear Lake
- Pasadena
- Missouri City
- Sugar Land
- South Houston
- Nearby surrounding areas

TARGET CATEGORIES:

1. APARTMENTS / MULTIFAMILY
Focus on:
- Garden-style apartments
- Class B/C properties preferred
- Approximately 80–300 units preferred
- Small-to-mid-sized operators
- Regional management companies

Avoid:
- Luxury downtown high-rises
- Student housing
- Senior living
- Hotels

2. COMMERCIAL PROPERTY MANAGEMENT
Focus on:
- Retail centers
- Office parks
- Mixed-use properties
- Small-to-mid local commercial property managers

Avoid:
- Huge national corporations if possible

3. FACILITY MANAGEMENT / FACILITY SERVICE COMPANIES
Focus on:
- Local/regional facility maintenance firms
- Janitorial companies
- Exterior maintenance vendors
- Building service contractors
- Companies likely to subcontract recurring work

4. HOA / COMMUNITY ASSOCIATION MANAGEMENT
Focus on:
- HOA management companies
- Community associations
- Townhome associations
- MUD/community maintenance organizations

5. LIGHT INDUSTRIAL / WAREHOUSE
Focus on:
- Light industrial
- Flex industrial
- Distribution centers
- Owner-managed warehouse properties
- Small-to-mid industrial parks

Avoid:
- Massive enterprise/national facilities

DATABASE REQUIREMENTS:

Please provide an organized spreadsheet with separate tabs/categories if possible.

REQUIRED COLUMNS:
- Company / Property Name
- Category
- Address
- City
- ZIP
- Website
- Phone Number
- Management Company
- Decision Maker Name
- Job Title
- Email Address
- LinkedIn Profile
- Estimated Property Size or Unit Count (if available)
- Notes
- Priority/Quality Notes

IMPORTANT FILTERING REQUIREMENTS:

Please prioritize:
- Small-to-mid-sized companies
- Local or regional operators
- Businesses likely to work with local vendors
- Companies/properties with recurring maintenance potential
- Older properties with likely exterior cleaning needs
- Companies managing under approximately 50 properties if possible

Please avoid:
- Generic homeowner leads
- Residential consumers
- National enterprise corporations requiring massive vendor onboarding
- Duplicate entries
- Low-quality or incomplete records

DATA SOURCING:

You may use:
- Google Maps
- LinkedIn
- Apollo.io
- Company websites
- Commercial real estate directories
- Public business/property records
- Other legal/professional sourcing methods

DELIVERABLE:
Clean, organized spreadsheet with high-quality Houston-area commercial maintenance prospects suitable for long-term business development and recurring service outreach.

For this project, I prioritize high-quality, well-targeted commercial/property management prospects over large quantities of generic leads. Please include your estimated number of verified records and explain your sourcing and verification process.

Please include examples/sample rows before final delivery if possible.

Please also explain:
- approximately how many records you expect to provide
- how you source decision maker information
- what tools/platforms you use
- expected turnaround time
questions: Have you built B2B/commercial property lead lists before? Please describe one similar project.
What tools will you use to find and verify leads?
Can you provide 5 sample rows before completing the full list?
How will you avoid duplicates and low-quality records?
For $100, approximately how many high-quality records can you provide?
body_json.title: Houston Commercial Property & Recurring Maintenance Prospect Database

body_json.description:
ShipIndia Ecommerce App – Functional Testing Brief
1. Project Background
ShipIndia is an ecommerce platform that allows Indian vendors to sell products to customers in Singapore and Malaysia.

The platform supports different users:

Customer
End user who browses ShipIndia, adds products to cart, pays online, tracks orders, and receives items overseas.

Vendor
Indian seller who lists products on ShipIndia and fulfils orders by sending items locally to the ShipIndia India warehouse.

There are two vendor types:

Shopify Owners
Vendors who already have their own Shopify store. They install the ShipIndia Shopify app, connect their store, and ShipIndia pulls their products/inventory into the ShipIndia marketplace. When a customer places an order on ShipIndia, the vendor receives the order in their Shopify/ShipIndia vendor panel and ships the item to ShipIndia’s India warehouse.

Non-Shopify Owners
Vendors who do not have a Shopify store. They use the ShipIndia vendor portal to manually upload products, update inventory, receive orders, and manage dispatches.

Logistics User
Internal logistics team login used to manage received parcels, warehouse processing, dispatch, export, shipment updates, and delivery status.

Admin
ShipIndia internal admin login used to manage customers, vendors, products, orders, approvals, payouts, settings, logistics flow, and platform-level controls.
2. Testing Objective
To complete comprehensive functional testing of the ShipIndia ecommerce platform within 3 days, covering customer, vendor, logistics, and admin flows.

The tester must identify:
• Broken links
• Functional errors
• Button/page issues
• Mobile alignment issues
• Visual inconsistencies
• Poor user flow
• Missing validation
• Email notification issues
• Order flow issues
• Vendor onboarding issues
• Usability gaps
• Recommended improvements

This is not security testing.
3. Scope of Testing
A. Customer Flow Testing
• Sign up / login
• OTP or email verification
• Browse products
• Search and filters
• Product page details
• Add to cart
• Checkout flow
• Payment process
• Order confirmation
• Email notifications
• Tracking updates
• Mobile responsiveness

B. Vendor Flow Testing

Shopify Vendor Flow
• Shopify app installation
• Vendor registration
• Company profile setup
• Product sync from Shopify
• Inventory sync
• Order notification
• Dispatch process
• Tracking upload
• Email notifications

Non-Shopify Vendor Flow
• Vendor registration
• Product upload
• Inventory update
• Order management
• Dispatch flow
• Tracking upload
• Email notifications

C. Logistics Flow Testing
• Logistics login
• Warehouse receiving
• Dispatch updates
• Tracking updates
• Dashboard usability

D. Admin Flow Testing
• Vendor approval
• Product approval
• Customer management
• Order management
• Dashboard usability
• Platform controls
4. Email Notification Testing
Tester must verify all email triggers including:
• Registration emails
• Order confirmation emails
• Dispatch notifications
• Warehouse received notifications
• Delivery confirmations
• Cancellation notifications

Tester must confirm:
• Email receipt
• Correct content
• Working links
• Branding consistency
• Mobile responsiveness
5. Mobile and Visual Testing
Tester must test:
• Desktop view
• Mobile browser view
• Tablet view

Check for:
• Alignment issues
• Button spacing
• Image scaling
• Menu usability
• Form responsiveness
• UI consistency
• Font consistency
• Broken UI sections
6. Required Deliverables
1. Functional Testing Report
Tester must provide a complete issue report with:
• Module
• User type
• Issue description
• Severity level
• Screenshot evidence
• Recommendations

2. Screenshot-Based Vendor Guide

For Shopify Vendors:
• Install ShipIndia app
• Connect Shopify store
• Enter company details
• Sync products
• Receive orders
• Dispatch orders
• Upload tracking

For Non-Shopify Vendors:
• Register vendor account
• Upload products
• Manage inventory
• Receive orders
• Dispatch orders
• Upload tracking

3. Usability Recommendation Report
Tester must suggest improvements for:
• Customer flow
• Vendor onboarding
• Checkout process
• Logistics flow
• Admin dashboard
• Mobile usability
7. Timeline
Day 1
• Customer flow
• Shopify vendor flow
• Email testing

Day 2
• Non-Shopify vendor flow
• Logistics flow
• Admin flow

Day 3
• Retesting
• Screenshot guide
• Final reporting
• Recommendations
8. Final Expected Outcome
By the end of testing, ShipIndia should receive:
• Complete bug and issue report
• Screenshot evidence
• Usability recommendations
• Vendor training guide
• Readiness assessment for launch
body_json.title: Testing for ecommerce platform

body_json.description:
Full-Time Bookkeeper / Accountant
We're a Kansas City–based holding company (Callidus Holdings) operating a portfolio of bars, restaurants, and two software ventures. We need a full-time bookkeeper/accountant to take ownership of day-to-day books across the group and partner closely with ownership and our CPA on month-end close, payroll reconciliation, and reporting.
Scope of work:
• Full-cycle bookkeeping across ~8 operating entities + 2 holding companies in QuickBooks Online
• Daily/weekly bank, credit card, and merchant processor reconciliations
• AP management, bill pay scheduling, vendor onboarding (W-9s, 1099 tracking)
• AR, intercompany transfers, and owner draw/distribution tracking across the holding structure
• Payroll reconciliation
• Monthly close, P&L by venue, balance sheet review, and cash flow reporting
• Sales tax filings (Missouri + Texas) and coordination with our CPA on quarterly/annual filings
• Document organization in Google Drive — keeping receipts, invoices, and statements tied to transactions
Must-haves:
• 3+ years bookkeeping
• Strong QBO expertise — class/location tracking across multiple entities
• Comfortable with tipped payroll and restaurant chart of accounts
• Self-directed, deadline-driven, clean documentation habits
• Fluent written English and responsive on Slack/email during US business hours (CST)
Commitment: ~40 hrs/week, ongoing. Start ASAP.
body_json.title: Staff Accountant

body_json.description:
DFW Emergency Response LLC is seeking a senior WordPress / Elementor developer or small development team to build a mobile-first emergency response operations platform.

This is not a basic contractor website.

We already have the business concept, service structure, visual direction, screenshots, content direction, and detailed platform requirements. The selected developer will not be starting from scratch creatively. We need someone who can take our direction and build the platform correctly on the technical side.

The platform must be elite, fast, mobile-first, and operationally functional.

The build must include:
• WordPress / Elementor development
• Mobile-first emergency response design
• Customer request intake
• Photo and video upload capability
• Click-to-call functionality
• Jobber workflow integration
• Vendor onboarding/application workflow
• Fast backend setup
• SEO-ready structure
• High-performance hosting recommendation
• Security, backups, and ownership handoff
• Future-ready architecture for portals, automation, CRM expansion, call tracking, reviews, and multi-city growth

Speed and performance are very important. The preferred mobile load speed target is 1.5 seconds or faster, with under 2 seconds as the maximum acceptable target.

We want a clean, scalable, future-proof build using widely supported technology. No proprietary lock-in. No bloated templates. No outdated plugins. No system that only one developer can maintain.

All accounts, website files, hosting, domain, uploaded data, customer data, vendor data, Google accounts, design assets, and administrative access must remain owned and controlled by DFW Emergency Response LLC.

A detailed specification PDF is attached. Please review it before responding.

In your response, please include:
• Relevant portfolio examples
• WordPress / Elementor experience
• Jobber or field-service integration experience
• How you would architect this platform
• Timeline estimate
• Fixed-price estimate
• Hosting recommendation
• Maintenance/support options after launch
• What would be included and excluded from your proposal

We are looking for someone who understands operational platforms, not just website design.
body_json.title: Senior WordPress/Elementor Developer Needed for Emergency Response Operations Platform

body_json.description:
Singapore-incorporated Pte Ltd needs a first-year statutory audit on a 5 business-day timeline. Engagement starts immediately.

Company: Software / cybersecurity sector. Singapore subsidiary of a US parent company. Incorporated mid-2025. First FYE 31 December 2025 (8-month stub period). Full company details shared with shortlisted candidates under NDA.

We qualify for the Singapore small-company audit exemption under section 205C of the Companies Act, but are commissioning a voluntary audit to satisfy an enterprise customer's vendor onboarding requirement.

Scope (genuinely small):

    - 8-month first FY (calendar 2025)
    - Singapore Financial Reporting Standards for Small Entities
    - Singapore Standards on Auditing (SSAs)
    - Approximately 60 bank transactions across 3 currencies
    - Total assets under SGD 500; total revenue under SGD 10,000
    - One customer-receivable invoice (documented with PO and contract)
    - Small US-parent intercompany capital injections (already classified as equity, documented in Notes)


Documents ready for Day 1 delivery (audit pack):

    - Reconciled Management Accounts (P&L + Balance Sheet) with line-by-line source notes
    - Wise bank statements (SGD, USD, IDR) in Excel and stamped PDF
    - Unaudited Annual Report draft (FYE 31 Dec 2025), SFRS for Small Entities basis
    - ACRA Bizfile, Company Extract, Constitution
    - All contractor W-9 / W-8BEN forms
    - NRA / OFAC / Principal Address position memos


Timeline (must commit in proposal):

    - Day 1 Mon: engagement letter signed, documents delivered
    - Day 5 Fri: signed audit opinion + audited FS PDF delivered


Required:
    - ACRA-registered Public Accountant or working for an ACRA-registered audit firm. Include PA registration number and firm ACRA registration.
    - ISCA member in good standing.
    - Confirmed capacity to start this ASAP.

IMPORTANT: Applicants without ACRA Public Accountant credentials will not be considered.
questions: Your or your firm's ACRA Public Accountant registration details (registration number and firm name).
Fastest realistic turnaround from start of fieldwork to signed opinion, given the scope above.
Have you audited a first-year Singapore Pte Ltd company in the last 12 months? If yes, brief description of the engagement (no client names needed).
body_json.title: Urgent: Singapore Statutory Audit (Pte Ltd, First FY) — Start ASAP

body_json.description:
# Lead Generation & Vendor Onboarding Specialist – Food/FMCG Ecosystem

## About Us

We are an AI-enabled B2B growth ecosystem platform focused on Food, FMCG, and MSMEs. The platform connects startups, vendors, distributors, and business enablers through advisory, ecosystem building, vendor discovery, and business matchmaking.

We are looking for a proactive Lead Generation & Vendor Onboarding Specialist to support ecosystem expansion and business growth.

---

## Role Overview

This role focuses on:

* generating and managing qualified leads,
* setting up lead capture systems,
* researching and onboarding vendors,
* supporting sales outreach,
* and helping build the Food India Story vendor ecosystem.

The role requires a combination of:

* lead generation,
* CRM management,
* vendor outreach,
* and ecosystem coordination.

---

## Key Responsibilities

### 1. Lead Generation & Lead Management

* Generate business leads through:

  * LinkedIn
  * Website inquiries
  * Social media
  * Industry directories
  * Online research
  * Ecosystem networking
  * Startup and university/faculty ecosystem
 * Maintain structured lead databases
* Track follow-ups and sales pipeline
* Support appointment scheduling and lead nurturing

### 2. Lead Capture System Setup

  * Help create and manage:
  * Website lead forms
  * Google Forms
  * CRM systems
  * WhatsApp lead collection workflows
  * Lead tracking sheets
  * AI-assisted automation workflows
  * Support integration of:
  * Zapier
  * Google Sheets
  * Email notifications
  * Social media lead workflows

### 3. Vendor Research & Onboarding

Research and onboard vendors related to:

* Food processing machinery
* Packaging materials
* Logistics providers
* Warehousing companies
* Ingredient suppliers
* Retail support vendors
* MSME ecosystem partners
* Statutory/FSSAI compliance service providers
---

### 4. Vendor Outreach & Relationship Building

  * Contact vendors through:
  * LinkedIn
  * Email
  * WhatsApp
  * Phone calls

* Explain platform benefits
* Coordinate onboarding process
* Maintain vendor records and follow-up   schedules

### 5. Sales Support & Target Achievement

 * Work on planned monthly targets for:
  * Leads generated
  * Vendor onboarding
  * Calls scheduled
  * Sales conversion support
  * Assist the founder in ecosystem expansion initiatives
## Preferred Skills

* Lead generation experience
* CRM and pipeline management
* LinkedIn outreach knowledge
* Strong communication skills
* Research-oriented mindset
* Familiarity with automation tools preferred
* FMCG/Food ecosystem understanding preferred

## Preferred Tools

* LinkedIn
* Google Sheets
* Zapier
* Canva (basic)
* Notion / Trello / CRM tools
* WhatsApp Business

## Performance Metrics

* Number of leads generated
* Quality of leads
* Vendor onboarding numbers
* Follow-up consistency
* Sales support effectiveness
* CRM accuracy preferred but not mandatory

## Engagement Type

* Freelance / Part-time
* Remote
* Initial 1-month engagement (extendable)

## To Apply

Please share:

1. Relevant experience
2. Lead generation tools/platforms used
3. Experience in vendor onboarding or outreach
4. Sample achievements/results
5. Availability per week
questions: Are you confident to handle this challenge of a startup ?
Describe your recent experience with similar projects
body_json.title: Lead Generation & Vendor Onboarding Specialist – Food/FMCG Ecosystem
